title: paeonia delavayi subsp. lutea
intro: Paeonia delavayi subsp. lutea is a subspecies of the peony plant, specifically a type of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of China and Tibet. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it useful for treating menstrual disorders, rheumatism, and other conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, promoting relaxation, and supporting liver function. Paeonia delavayi subsp. lutea can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
